title: asplenium yoshinagae var. indicum
intro: Asplenium yoshinagae var. indicum, also known as the Japanese spleenwort or Himalayan spleenwort, is a type of fern species belonging to the Aspleniaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. In herbal preparations, Asplenium yoshinagae var. indicum is often used in the form of dried leaves or powder, and can be consumed as a decoction or mixed with other herbs to create a topical paste for skin conditions.
